titleStmt.title: SII 33.87: original edition by K. Munirathnam and T. S. Ravishankar
No. 87. (A.R. No. 9 of 1948.) Jūṭūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.

summary.p: Stone set up in the Chennakēśava temple.
This is dated Śaka 1449, expressed in chronogram nava-vārdhi-vēda-śaśi Sarvajit, Chaitra ba. 6, Ina-sūnu-vāra corresponding to 1527 A.D., March 23, Saturday °70.

Records the gift of some lands as Sarvamānya in the village Pena-juṭūru situated in the Gutti-sīṁa, for conducting different kinds of rituals associated with the processional deities of the gods Kēśava and Paramēśvara by Daḷavāyi Timma-Māhēśvara, brother of Kṛishṇama Nāyaka.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrīmacchakābdē navavā¨rdhivēdē
śaśāṁka saṁjñākadhi tāsnihai
vasuśōbhanē sa¨rvajidabdikēdya cai
trē ca ṣaṣṭhyā minasūnuvārē . śaśā
ṁkabhē paṁcama vaṁśa saṁbhavō
bhaṁḍāru caṁnnakṣitipasya naṁda
naḥ . kṛṣṇānujāstiṁma mahī
śvarō sāvādiprabhōsyāddaḷavāIsaṁjñakaḥ .
rinagaṁḍa śavasya śivasya ca . nṛtyauni tāḍita
vibhava śāsanaṁ kṛtavān prabhuḥ . śrīma
drājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīra
pratāpa śrīkṛṣṇadēvarāya mahārāya
lu Ākṛṣṇanāyani gārikini pā Ānagā
nu tama guttisīmalōni penajūṭūru ta
kṛṣṇamanāyaniki pāliṁcukuṁṭurā
nu Ākriṣṇama nāya vaṁśastuḍu tiṁmmappanā
yaḍu yī jūṭūri kēśavarāyanikinni pa
ramēśvarunikinni Aṁgaraṁga vayibhavālu sēyi
ṁci yīśvaruniki maḍikiṁgānu Ācceḍi śrō
traya rokhamu sarvamānyamu sēsi pōgālaru
yī sthānālu reṁṭikiṁjelleṭivitri(vrittula)
lōnu sthānāla vāru reḍlu karaṇāla muṁda
ranu saṁmatiṁci Iccina maḍi kha 0 . 3 padu
mu tu . cenu kha 1 puṭṭeḍu nēmu Icci
na maḍi 93 muttumu cenu kha 0 . 0 paṁdumu
nu voṁṭa 91 ḍū mekunnu kū¨rciyisti
mi yī kṣētrānaku sa¨rvamānyaṁ rokhāna
ku yī vaibhavānaku yevvaru vighnaṁ sēya
niṁcinānu vāraṇāsilōnu
gohatya brahāhatyalu sēsina pāpāna bō
vuduru . svadattādiguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ pa
radatānu pālanaṁ paradattāpahā
rēṇi svadattaṁ niṣpalaṁ bhavet .
Digital edition of SII 33.87 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
